title: How Much Does a Kitchen Renovation Cost in Sydney?
metaTitle: Kitchen Renovation Cost Sydney — What to Expect in 2025 | InsideOut Joinery
metaDescription: Real kitchen renovation costs in Sydney from a licensed contractor. What drives price up, what keeps it down, and how to get a result worth paying for.
heroAlt: Completed custom kitchen renovation in Sydney — handleless cabinetry and stone benchtop
excerpt: Kitchen renovation costs in Sydney vary significantly depending on layout, materials and scope. Here is an honest breakdown from a contractor who does this work every week.

sections.heading: The honest answer is: it depends on what you are actually doing
sections.body: The range quoted online for Sydney kitchen renovations is usually $15,000 to $80,000 or more. That range is not wrong but it is not useful either. A kitchen renovation that involves pulling out existing cabinetry, installing new joinery in the same footprint, with standard appliances and a stone benchtop is a fundamentally different job to one that moves plumbing, relocates a wall, installs a full suite of premium appliances and specifies high-end stone throughout. The price difference between those two jobs is real and large. What actually matters is understanding which job your kitchen needs and what is driving the cost.

sections.heading: Entry-level kitchen renovations — $15,000 to $25,000
sections.body: At this price point you are typically looking at a supply and install of new cabinetry in an existing layout, a stone or laminate benchtop, a new sink and tap, and a basic appliance package. The footprint does not change, plumbing stays where it is, and the existing electrical is largely left alone. This is a legitimate renovation that produces a genuinely better kitchen — particularly if the original joinery was old, tired or poorly planned. The limitation is that it works best when the existing layout is fundamentally sound. If the layout is the problem, spending $20,000 to install new cabinetry in a bad configuration is not money well spent.

sections.heading: Mid-range kitchen renovations — $25,000 to $45,000
sections.body: This is where most serious kitchen renovations in Sydney sit. At this level you are getting custom cabinetry built to the exact dimensions of your space — no fillers, no wasted corners, no standard-size cabinets that almost fit. Stone benchtops, quality tapware, a proper appliance package, and usually some layout adjustment to improve how the kitchen functions. The joinery is built specifically for your room. An island is usually achievable at this price point if the footprint supports it. This is also where 3D design becomes important — seeing the layout in three dimensions before anything is ordered or demolished prevents expensive decisions later.

sections.heading: Premium kitchen renovations — $45,000 and above
sections.body: At the premium end you are looking at complex layouts, high-specification appliances, natural stone or engineered stone throughout, and a higher level of finish and detailing across the joinery. This might include things like integrated appliances behind custom panels, a full butler's pantry scope, handleless joinery with hidden finger-pull detailing, or a full home joinery language that carries through from the kitchen to supporting rooms. Projects like the Willoughby renovation — which included kitchen, laundry, mudroom, wardrobes and vanities planned as one connected fitout — sit at this level. The premium price reflects the scope, the materials and the planning required to make it work properly.

sections.heading: What actually drives the cost up
sections.body: Layout changes are expensive because they involve trades. Moving plumbing, relocating a window, removing a wall — these all require licensed trades and sometimes structural work before the joinery even starts. Appliance quality is a significant variable. A basic cooking and refrigeration package might cost $3,000. A premium cooking suite with an integrated fridge and a proper rangehood can add $15,000 to $20,000 to the job. Stone selection matters too — standard engineered stone and a Calacatta marble-look premium slab are very different price points. Material choices across the cabinetry finish, hardware, and splashback all contribute. The honest truth is that most kitchens end up costing more than the initial number because people add things they want once the scope is in front of them.

sections.heading: What you can do to control the cost
sections.body: The single most effective way to control kitchen renovation cost is to get the layout right before selecting any finishes. If the layout is wrong, no finish will fix it. Start with how the kitchen needs to function — bench space, storage, workflow, appliance positions — and work outward from there. A 3D design render before anything is ordered lets you see exactly what you are buying and change it before it becomes expensive. We include this with every quote. Trade-cost appliances are another lever — we buy appliances at trade pricing and pass the saving through, which can reduce the appliance budget meaningfully on a mid-range or premium scope.

sections.heading: What to ask before you commit to a quote
sections.body: Ask what is included and what is not. Some quotes include appliances, some do not. Some include trades coordination, some expect you to manage your own plumber and electrician. Ask whether the cabinetry is custom-built to your dimensions or standard boxes with fillers. Ask whether the quote covers removal and disposal of the existing kitchen. Ask for a 3D render or detailed drawings before you sign anything. And ask about the timeline — a realistic kitchen renovation takes three to five weeks from start to finish. If you are being quoted a week, ask what that actually means.

ctaTitle: Want a straight quote for your kitchen renovation?
ctaText: Send through your plans, photos or a rough description and we can give you a realistic scope and price range based on what the kitchen actually needs.
